DEFENDANT RECOLOGY, INC.'s Motion To Compel Production Of Medical Records And Authorizations; And Request For Monetary Sanctions.

The Court modifies the ruling on sanctions. The Court imposes $5,000 sanctions against Mr. Hernandez but not against Mr. Feder's office

Hearing is held. ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows: (SEE WORD MINUTES FOR FULL DETAILS)

DEFENDANT RECOLOGY, INC'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Defendants Interrogatories And For Sanctions

Recology's motion to compel further responses to the interrogatories identified in its moving and supplementary pleadings, save for its dissatisfaction with plaintiff's responses to punitive damage inquiries, is GRANTED. Also GRANTED is Recology's motion to compel a further response to the several requests identified in its moving papers, together with the actual production of those documents, including plaintiff's social media posts. However, given the bumpy course of the communications between counsel, Recology's request for an award of sanctions is DENIED.

As the prevailing party, Recology is directed to prepare an order specifying the interrogatories and document requests to which the plaintiff has been ordered to provide further verified responses, as well as the responsive documents themselves. The order shall be in accordance with the foregoing in compliance with Rule 3.1312. Once the order has been approved by plaintiff's counsel as to form, it should be forwarded to psw@hassard.com to be reviewed and signed.

Judge Pro Tem: Philip Ward; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/JPT)

Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepar85@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
